volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 3.1.470 - Section 3.2.550b)

sections.label: Section 3.1.470
sections.original: Per alium quemvis ut des ; falli te sinas
sections.translation: supply him with money through some other person ; suffer yourself to be imposed upon by the artifices of his servant : although I have smelt out this too , that they are about that , and are secretly planning it among them . Syrus is always whispering with that servant of yours ; [*] they impart their plans to the young men ; and it were better for you to lose

sections.label: Section 3.1.475
sections.original: Talentum hoc pacto satius est quam illo minam .
sections.translation: a talent this way , than a mina the other . The money is not the question now , but this — in what way we can supply it to the young man with the least danger . For if he once knows the state of your feelings , that you would sooner part with your life , and sooner

sections.label: Section 3.1.480
sections.original: Pecuniam omnem , quam abs te amittas filium ; hui ,
sections.translation: with all your money , than allow your son to leave you ; whew ! what an inlet [*] will you be opening for his debauchery ! aye , and so much so , that henceforth to live can not be desirable to you . For we all become worse through indulgence . Whatever comes into his head , he ’ ll be wishing for ; nor

sections.label: Section 3.1.485
sections.original: Putabit , pravum siet an rectum quod petet .
sections.translation: will he reflect whether that which he desires is right or wrong . You will not be able to endure your estate and him going to ruin . You will refuse to supply him : he will immediately have recourse to the means by which he finds that he has the greatest hold upon you , and threaten that he will immediately leave you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.490
sections.original: Videre verum , atque ita uti res est , dicere .
sections.translation: You seem to speak the truth , and just what is the fact .

sections.label: Section 3.1.491
sections.original: Somnum hercle ego hac nocte oculis non vidi meis ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I have not been sensible of sleep this night with my eyes , [*] for thinking of this — how to restore your son to you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.493
sections.original: Cedo dextram : porro te idem oro ut facias , Chreme .
sections.translation: Give me your right hand . I request that you will still act in a like manner , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 3.1.494
sections.original: Paratus sum .
sections.translation: I am ready to serve you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.494b
sections.original: Scin quid nunc facere te volo ?
sections.translation: Do you know what it is I now want you to do ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.495
sections.original: Dic .
sections.translation: Tell me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.495b
sections.original: Quod sensisti illos me incipere fallere .
sections.translation: As you have perceived that they are laying a plan to deceive me , that they may hasten to complete it . I long to give him whatever he wants : I am now longing to behold him .

sections.label: Section 3.1.497b
sections.original: Operam dabo .
sections.translation: I ’ ll lend my endeavors . This little business is in my way . Our neighbors Simus and Crito are disputing here about boundaries ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.500
sections.original: Me cepere arbitrum : ibo ac dicam , ut dixeram
sections.translation: they have chosen me for arbitrator . I ’ ll go and tell them that I can not possibly give them my attention to-day as I had stated I would . I ’ ll be here immediately .

sections.label: Section 3.1.502b
sections.original: Ita quaeso . Di vestram fidem !
sections.translation: Pray do . ( To himself .) Ye Gods , by our trust in you ! That the nature of all men should be so constituted , that they can see and judge of other men ’ s affairs better

sections.label: Section 3.1.505
sections.original: Quam sua ? an eo fit quia in re nostra aut gaudio
sections.translation: than their own ! Is it because in our own concerns we are biased either with joy or grief in too great a degree ? How much wiser now is he for me , than I have been for myself !

sections.label: Section 3.1.508
sections.original: Dissolvi me otiosus operam ut tibi darem .
sections.translation: I have disengaged myself , that I might lend you my services at my leisure . Syrus must be found and instructed by me in this business .

sections.label: Section 3.1.510
sections.original: A me nescio quis exit : concede hinc domum ,
sections.translation: Some one , I know not who , is coming out of my house : do you step hence home , that they may not perceive [*] that we are conferring together .

sections.label: Section 3.2.512
sections.original: Hac illac circumcursa : inveniendum est tamen
sections.translation: Run to and fro in every direction ; still , money , you must be found : a trap must be laid for the old man .

sections.label: Section 3.2.514
sections.original: Num me fefellit hosce id struere ? Videlicet
sections.translation: Was I deceived in saying that they were planning this ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.515
sections.original: Quia Cliniae ille servus tardiusculus est ,
sections.translation: That servant of Clinia ’ s is somewhat dull ; therefore that province has been assigned to this one of ours .

sections.label: Section 3.2.517
sections.original: Quis hic loquitur ? Perii . Numnam haec audivit ?
sections.translation: Who ’ s that speaking ? ( Catches sight of CGREMES .) I ’ m undone ! Did he hear it , I wonder ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.517b
sections.original: Syre .
sections.translation: Syrus .

sections.label: Section 3.2.517c
sections.original: Hem .
sections.translation: Well —

sections.label: Section 3.2.518
sections.original: Quid tu istic ?
sections.translation: What are you doing here ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.518b
sections.original: Recte equidem : sed te miror , Chreme ,
sections.translation: All right . Really , I am quite surprised at you , Chremes , up so early , after drinking so much yesterday .

sections.label: Section 3.2.519b
sections.original: Nihil nimis .
sections.translation: Not too much .

sections.label: Section 3.2.520
sections.original: Nihil narras ? visa vero est , quod dici solet ,
sections.translation: Not too much , say you ? Really , you ’ ve seen the old age of an eagle , [*] as the saying is .

sections.label: Section 3.2.521b
sections.original: Heia .
sections.translation: Pooh , pooh !

sections.label: Section 3.2.521c
sections.original: Mulier commoda et
sections.translation: A pleasant and agreeable woman this Courtesan .

sections.label: Section 3.2.522b
sections.original: Sane , idem visa est mihi .
sections.translation: Why , so she seemed to me , in fact .

sections.label: Section 3.2.523
sections.original: Et quidem hercle forma luculenta .
sections.translation: And really of handsome appearance .

sections.label: Section 3.2.523b
sections.original: Sic satis .
sections.translation: Well enough .

sections.label: Section 3.2.524
sections.original: Ita non ut olim ; sed uti nunc , sane bona :
sections.translation: Not like those of former days , [*] but as times are now , very passable :

sections.label: Section 3.2.525
sections.original: Minimeque miror Clinia hanc si deperit .
sections.translation: nor do I in the least wonder that Clinia doats upon her . But he has a father — a certain covetous , miserable , and niggardly person — this neighbor of ours ( pointing to the house ) . Do you know him ? Yet , as if he was not abounding in wealth , his son ran away through want . Are you aware that it is the fact , as I am saying ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.529b
sections.original: Quid ego nesciam ?
sections.translation: How should I not be aware ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.530
sections.original: Hominem pistrino dignum .
sections.translation: A fellow that deserves the mill .

sections.label: Section 3.2.530b
sections.original: Quem ?
sections.translation: Who ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.530c
sections.original: Istunc servulum
sections.translation: That servant of the young gentleman , I mean .

sections.label: Section 3.2.531b
sections.original: Syre , tibi timui male .
sections.translation: Syrus ! I was sadly afraid for you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.532
sections.original: Qui passus est id fieri .
sections.translation: To suffer it to come to this !

sections.label: Section 3.2.532b
sections.original: Quid faceret ?
sections.translation: What was he to do ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.532c
sections.original: Rogas ?
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? He ought to have found some expedient , contrived some stratagem , by means of which there might have been something for the young man to give to his mistress ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.535
sections.original: Atque hunc difficilem invitum servaret senem .
sections.translation: and thus have saved this crabbed old fellow in spite of himself .

sections.label: Section 3.2.536
sections.original: Garris .
sections.translation: You are surely joking .

sections.label: Section 3.2.536b
sections.original: Haec facta ab illo oportebat , Syre .
sections.translation: This ought to have been done by him , Syrus .

sections.label: Section 3.2.537
sections.original: Eho quaeso , laudas qui heros fallunt ?
sections.translation: How now — pray , do you commend servants , who deceive their masters ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.537b
sections.original: In loco
sections.translation: Upon occasion — I certainly do commend them .

sections.label: Section 3.2.538b
sections.original: Recte sane .
sections.translation: Quite right .

sections.label: Section 3.2.538c
sections.original: Quippe quia
sections.translation: Inasmuch as it often is the remedy for great disturbances .

sections.label: Section 3.2.540
sections.original: Huic iam mansisset unicus gnatus domi .
sections.translation: Then would this man ’ s only son have staid at home .

sections.label: Section 3.2.541
sections.original: Iocone an serio illaec dicat , nescio ;
sections.translation: Whether he says this in jest or in earnest , I don ’ t know ; only , in fact , that he gives me additional zest for longing still more to trick him .

sections.label: Section 3.2.543
sections.original: Et nunc quid exspectat , Syre ? an dum hinc denuo
sections.translation: And what is he now waiting for , Syrus ? Is it until his father drives him away from here a second time , when he can no longer support her expenses ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.2.545
sections.original: Nonne ad senem aliquam fabricam fingit ?
sections.translation: Has he no plot on foot against the old gentleman ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.545b
sections.original: Stolidus est .
sections.translation: He is a stupid fellow .

sections.label: Section 3.2.546
sections.original: At te adiutare oportet adolescentuli
sections.translation: Then you ought to assist him — for the sake of the young man .

sections.label: Section 3.2.547b
sections.original: Facile equidem facere possum , si iubes :
sections.translation: For my part , I can do so easily , if you command me ; for I know well in what fashion it is usually done .

sections.label: Section 3.2.549
sections.original: Tanto hercle melior .
sections.translation: So much the better , i ’ faith .

sections.label: Section 3.2.549b
sections.original: Non est mentiri meum .
sections.translation: ’ Tis not my way to tell an untruth .

sections.label: Section 3.2.550
sections.original: Fac ergo .
sections.translation: Do it then .

sections.label: Section 3.2.550b
sections.original: At heus tu , facitodum eadem haec memineris ,
sections.translation: But hark you ! Just take care and remember this , in case any thing of this sort should perchance happen at a future time , such are human affairs !— your son might do the same .
